beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check ferry schedule and weather forecast.
beats.reasoning: Diego wakes early as always, obsessively checking the ferry schedule—heat wave and outdoor concert will drive crowds, so he needs to plan commute carefully. Low energy yesterday (45/100) suggests he'll keep morning routine calm and deliberate.

beats.activity: Ferry commute from Mercer Island to Downtown Seattle.
beats.reasoning: Early ferry avoids the heat-day crowds and gets him to the stall before the worst of the midday sun; Diego tracks ferries obsessively and would time this precisely.

beats.activity: Arrange and sell flowers at Pike Place stall; prep for heat by focusing on hardy blooms and indoor displays.
beats.reasoning: Diego opens his stall early to beat the afternoon heat spike (91F by mid-afternoon). Heat and smoke will make outdoor work uncomfortable, so he'll prep cooler arrangements and stay in the market's shaded areas; regular customers like Mrs. Chen keep him grounded.

beats.activity: Lunch at usual spot in Pike Place Market; grab water to stay hydrated in heat.
beats.reasoning: Same sandwich routine, but Diego adds extra water to combat the 91-degree heat and smoke; he stays put rather than wandering in the oppressive afternoon sun.

beats.activity: Restock and tidy stall; take breaks in shade as heat peaks.
beats.reasoning: Diego stays at the market through the worst heat, working indoors/in shade. He avoids the 91F peak by remaining stationary; frugal nature means he won't close early just for weather, but he'll pace himself.

beats.activity: Close up stall and walk to Gas Works Park.
beats.reasoning: Diego walks from Pike Place to Gas Works Park to catch the 6pm outdoor concert; it's a pleasant evening walk as the heat eases, and he values time out but avoids big crowds by arriving early to secure a quiet spot.

beats.activity: Enjoy the free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park; sit quietly away from main crowd.
beats.reasoning: Diego appreciates live music (given his vinyl passion) and the cooling evening air; he avoids big crowds by positioning himself on the park's quieter edges, valuing solitude and good sound over social immersion.

beats.activity: Walk to Wallingford neighborhood and grab dinner at a small local spot.
beats.reasoning: After the concert, Diego walks into Wallingford proper to find a quiet dinner spot; frugal but willing to treat himself after a hot day, and he prefers neighborhood joints over chains.

beats.activity: Walk back toward ferry, taking a scenic route along the canal.
beats.reasoning: Diego takes his time heading back to catch an evening ferry, enjoying the cooler post-sunset air and reflecting on the day; no rush, just his routine rhythm.

beats.activity: Ferry commute from Downtown Seattle back to Mercer Island.
beats.reasoning: Evening ferry is quieter; Diego values this peaceful water time to decompress after a hot day and a concert crowd.

beats.activity: Home wind-down: put on a vinyl record (maybe some cool jazz or Lou Reed), have a light snack, prepare for bed.
beats.reasoning: Diego settles into his vinyl collection routine, a cherished ritual; he's already home and in no rush, just decompressing after the heat and outdoor socializing.
Diego gets a full night's rest after an eventful day; sleep spans overnight as required, ending well before dawn of the next calendar day.

world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
